channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Chemical leak at Dinmore Meatworks sends five to hospital and prompts mass evacuation
item.description: Early on Thursday morning, emergency services responded to a major chemical leak at the Dinmore Meatworks in Riverview, roughly 30 kilometres south-west of Brisbane. The incident, which involved a sig...

item.title: Tinubu and Kagame agree to strengthen Nigeria-Rwanda trade and cooperation
item.description: President Bola Tinubu don waka go Rwanda to meet President Paul Kagame, wey dem discuss how dem go take deepen relations between Nigeria and Rwanda. Dem gree to revive the Joint Permanent Ministerial ...

item.title: Meghalaya government assures farmers’ safety with additional forces at Assam border
item.description: Meghalaya Deputy Chief Minister Sniawbhalang Dhar on Wednesday assured farmers and activists from Lapangap village, West Jaiñtia Hills, that the state government will deploy additional security person...

item.title: Canada’s Men’s Soccer Team Highlights Multicultural Identity Ahead of 2026 World Cup
item.description: Canada’s participation in the 2026 Men’s World Cup is poised to offer a distinctive representation of national identity, distinct from traditional sporting symbols like hockey. While hockey has histor...

item.title: Army Soldier and Militiaman Killed in Sagay City Ambush
item.description: In Sagay City, Negros Occidental, an Army corporal and a militiaman were fatally shot by two men riding a motorcycle on Tuesday. Cpl. Robert Dador, 48, assistant detachment commander, and Johnery Monc...

item.title: Paul Natkin’s 1982 Portraits of Girlschool Capture Early NWOBHM Style
item.description: In April 1982, renowned photographer Paul Natkin photographed the British heavy metal band Girlschool during their visit to Chicago, Illinois. His sessions captured both staged promotional portraits a...

item.title: Fire damages North Adelaide Golf Course, home of 2027 Australian Open
item.description: A significant fire has engulfed the North Adelaide Golf Course, recently announced as the venue for the 2027 Australian Open, causing major damage just a week after the announcement. The course, which...

item.title: Profile and current life of Brian Timmons, Maya Brady’s biological father
item.description: Brian Timmons is the biological father of Maya Brady, who is now a professional softball player for the Oklahoma City Spark and was previously a standout player for the UCLA Bruins. Timmons and Maya's...

item.title: Lanre Adediwura Opens Up About Financial Struggles After Acting in ‘Wura’
item.description: Nollywood actor, Lanre Adediwura, don talk say him finish work for MultiChoice TV series 'Wura' dey financially shake. For interview with TVC Entertainment, Lanre yarn why him no fit accept to return ...

item.title: Edie Sedgwick: Life, Style, and Influence as Andy Warhol’s Muse
item.description: Edie Sedgwick, born Edith Minturn Sedgwick in 1943, was a defining figure of 1960s fashion and culture. Coming from a prominent New England family, she rose to fame upon entering Andy Warhol's Factory...

item.title: Erin Patterson joins general prison population at Dame Phyllis Frost Centre
item.description: Erin Patterson, infamously known as the 'mushroom killer', has been moved out of solitary confinement at Melbourne’s Dame Phyllis Frost Centre, where she is serving a minimum 33-year sentence for the ...

item.title: Tilly Tizzani: Iconic Italian Model of 1960s Fashion
item.description: Tilly Tizzani (1938–1975) was a leading figure in 1960s Italian fashion, celebrated for her striking beauty, elegant demeanor, and distinctive facial features. Rising quickly to international fame, sh...

item.title: CRC Credit Bureau wins top West African Innovation Award again
item.description: CRC Credit Bureau Limited don win the Diamond Award for Best Credit Bureau of the Year for the West African Innovation Awards 2026. This na the second year dem don carry this top honour back-to-back, ...

item.title: Explaining the Anti-Defection Law in the Context of AIADMK's Internal Dispute
item.description: The AIADMK in Tamil Nadu has witnessed a significant internal conflict as party general secretary Edappadi K. Palaniswami removed 26 district secretaries, including 12 legislators, amid growing factio...

item.title: Cenovus CEO argues carbon pricing alone won't reduce emissions in oil and gas industry
item.description: Cenovus Energy Inc. CEO Jon McKenzie has stated that carbon pricing alone will not drive the Canadian oil and gas sector to lower greenhouse gas emissions. Speaking at an Intersect event in Calgary, M...

item.title: Erik Fleming sentenced for supplying ketamine linked to Matthew Perry's death
item.description: Erik Fleming, a rehabilitation counsellor, has been sentenced to two years in prison for supplying ketamine to actor Matthew Perry before his death. Perry, the 54-year-old star of the TV show Friends,...

item.title: DCI Investigates Armed Gang in Nakuru Reverend’s Murder
item.description: Nairobi — The Directorate of Criminal Investigations (DCI) has linked a criminal gang to the murder of Reverend Julius Ndumia in Nakuru County. The incident happened on the night of Sunday, May 3, 202...

item.title: Trump’s Fed Chair Nominee Kevin Warsh Faces Political Challenges Amid Rising Inflation
item.description: Kevin Warsh, nominated by President Donald Trump to lead the Federal Reserve, was confirmed by the Senate with a narrow 54-45 vote, marking one of the most divisive confirmations in modern history. Wa...

item.title: National Council Takes Legal Action Against Durban Clothing Manufacturer Over Labour Violations
item.description: The National Bargaining Council for the Clothing Manufacturing Industry has initiated court proceedings against Durban-based fashion supplier Gemelli Pty Ltd, alleging it contracts with sweatshops dis...

item.title: VP Sara Duterte expresses support for Bato dela Rosa amid Senate controversy
item.description: Vice President Sara Duterte expressed her support for Senator Ronald “Bato” dela Rosa amid recent tensions in the Senate. Speaking from The Hague, Duterte said she was saddened and angered by the perc...
